title: Fever and New Murmur - Infective Endocarditis
chief_complaint: Fever 39°C for 2 weeks, night sweats, fatigue, and new-onset shortness of breath. Known mitral valve prolapse. Recent dental procedure without prophylaxis.

physical_exam.heart: New holosystolic murmur at apex radiating to axilla, grade 4/6
physical_exam.skin: Janeway lesions on palms, splinter hemorrhages in nails

hidden_data.echo: Large vegetation on mitral valve (1.5cm) with severe mitral regurgitation

patient_responses.fever: It comes and goes, mostly at night
patient_responses.dental: I had a tooth extraction 3 weeks ago, no antibiotics
patient_responses.breathing: I get short of breath walking to the bathroom

hints.history: Recent dental procedure without prophylaxis is key
hints.diagnosis: Apply Duke criteria - major and minor

feedback.correct_message: Excellent diagnosis, Doctor. IE with classic peripheral signs.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is infective endocarditis. The fever + new murmur + dental procedure + positive blood cultures = Duke criteria met.
feedback.attending_note: IE management: Prolonged IV antibiotics (4-6 weeks) guided by sensitivities. Surgical consultation for large vegetation (>1cm), severe regurgitation, or heart failure. Remember prophylaxis for high-risk patients before dental procedures.
